clinical_coherence.notes: 67M Hispanic retired engineer with 2.5-year progressive right-sided resting tremor, bradykinesia, rigidity, micrographia, reduced arm swing, stooped posture. Hyposmia and constipation as non-motor features. Vitals appropriate (mild HTN). Detailed neurological exam with all classic PD features.

tool_output_consistency.notes: Normal MRI with mild age-appropriate small vessel disease. Labs normal except borderline low Hgb and borderline high Na (146). DaTscan asymmetric left putaminal loss contralateral to right-side symptoms. Robust 50% levodopa response. Normal autonomic testing. PSG shows RBD (18% RWA). All concordant.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G20 correct. Excellent differential including ET, drug-induced, MSA, PSP, NPH. Comprehensive optimal actions with levodopa as first-line for age >65 supported by LEAP and PD MED trials. Contraindicated actions well-chosen including avoiding dopamine agonist monotherapy in elderly.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Clear-cut PD with asymmetric presentation, robust levodopa response, and no atypical features. Perfectly straightforward.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 67M with HTN, BPH, knee OA, mother with possible undiagnosed tremor. Highly realistic.

educational_value.notes: Good teaching case with emphasis on current evidence-based treatment (LEAP trial, PD MED). Literature search outputs add educational depth. However, very similar to S01/S03.

issues_found: Full Scale IQ 111 and Verbal Comprehension 116 marked as 'is_abnormal: true' in neuropsych but these are above-average scores, not abnormal
strengths: Evidence-based treatment discussion with trial references
Includes hyposmia as early PD feature
